key_reasoning: Outside view first: in the historical record, there has never been a publicly-attributed, meaningfully disruptive Iranian cyber attack on German critical infrastructure. The closest analogue is Albania 2022 (government IT, not CI; Albania directly hosted MEK opposition). The annual base rate for any given Western European country across the past decade approaches zero. Inside-view adjustment upward for active conflict: Iran is under unprecedented conventional pressure (70% launcher attrition), IRGC cyber command is actively developing new tooling during the war (PWL-026: MuddyWater's 'Dindoor' backdoor deployed in US networks, elevated wiper risk per Unit 42 as of late March 2026), and Iran's asymmetric options are expanding as conventional capacity degrades. But the scenario requires a compound of four independent conditions: (1) Iran decides Germany is a priority target despite it being a non-combatant with no direct role in the conflict, (2) pre-positioned OT/ICS access exists or can be acquired within 30 days, (3) the attack achieves the 'meaningful disruption' threshold against defended German CI, and (4) Germany publicly attributes. Germany is not a direct adversary, has strong defences (BSI), and Iran has strategic incentives to avoid drawing a major NATO/EU economy into direct opposition. The compound probability across all four conditions holds the estimate near 4%, with the attribution requirement (German government naming Iran publicly vs. quietly expelling diplomats) being an additional multiplicative discount.

framework_specific.reference_class: Publicly-attributed destructive/disruptive Iranian cyber operations against Western European critical infrastructure: ~0 confirmed incidents in 10-year historical record. Albanian government IT 2022 is the nearest analogue but differs on target class (government IT vs. CI) and strategic context (Albania hosted MEK).

fermi_decomposition.conflict_elevation_multiplier: ~3x applied for active-conflict asymmetric pressure, yielding ~0.04

framework_specific.bayesian_chain: Prior (peacetime base rate for 30-day window): ~0.1%. Evidence set: (a) PWL-026 confirms active IRGC cyber command development and elevated wiper risk as of March 2026 — LR ~2x upward; (b) MuddyWater operations focused on US networks, not German CI — LR ~0.5x downward; (c) Iran transmitting 14-point diplomatic response via Pakistan on Day 72, suggesting active diplomatic track — LR ~0.8x downward; (d) Germany non-combatant, no direct role in conflict — LR ~0.5x downward; (e) Iranian CI attacks historically confined to Middle East region — LR ~0.6x downward. Net posterior: ~0.04.
framework_specific.compound_requirements_penalty: Attribution requirement is an independent additional constraint: Germany has attributed state cyber attacks publicly (Russia/Bundestag 2015) but in a politically costly manner. In an active conflict, public Iran attribution would be a major diplomatic step. Estimated at 40% conditional on a disruptive attack — this alone suppresses the compound probability significantly.
key_uncertainties: Whether IRGC cyber command has pre-positioned OT/ICS access in German energy/rail networks (unknown; if yes, probability jumps significantly)
Whether German government would attribute publicly vs. privately expelling Iranian diplomats
Whether Iran views a German CI attack as advancing or undermining its diplomatic position
anti_patterns_checked: Inside-view dominance: controlled by anchoring on historical base rate first
Scope insensitivity: the Albanian 2022 precedent is genuinely different from German CI — not the same reference class
Round-number bias: 4% chosen over 5% after decomposition yielded ~3-4% range
framework_specific.what_would_change_assessment: Upward revision to 10-15% if: (a) Germany openly provides ISR or basing support to US forces; (b) confirmed evidence of IRGC OT/ICS pre-positioning in German utilities appears in intelligence; (c) Iran conducts a disruptive CI attack on another NATO country (establishing precedent). Downward revision to <1% if: Iran and US reach the MoU framework currently being negotiated.
